items.title: We’ve identified industrial-scale distillation attacks on our models by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and M
items.raw_summary:
We’ve identified industrial-scale distillation attacks on our models by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax.

These labs created over 24,000 fraudulent accounts and generated over 16 million exchanges with Claude, extracting its capabilities to train and improve their own models.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic identified industrial-scale distillation attacks by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax — 24K fake accounts, 16M exchanges — exposing a critical new threat vector for frontier model providers.
items.action: Read the full distillation attack report

items.title: New in Claude Code: Remote Control.
items.raw_summary:
New in Claude Code: Remote Control.

Kick off a task in your terminal and pick it up from your phone while you take a walk or join a meeting. 

Claude keeps running on your machine, and you can control the session from the Claude app or https://t.co/er6Blrr63e https://t.co/FxUVDe
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code Remote Control lets developers start terminal sessions locally and continue from mobile — a new paradigm for async agentic coding workflows.
items.action: Update Claude Code and try /remote-control

items.title: We're updating our Responsible Scaling Policy to its third version.
items.raw_summary:
We're updating our Responsible Scaling Policy to its third version.

Since it came into effect in 2023, we’ve learned a lot about the RSP’s benefits and its shortcomings. This update improves the policy, reinforcing what worked and committing us to even greater transparency.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic's RSP v3.0 overhauls its flagship AI safety framework with greater transparency commitments, while Time reports the move as 'dropping' the old pledge — a significant policy shift either way.
items.action: Read the RSP v3.0 and the Time critique side by side

items.raw_summary:

Today we’re announcing a multi-year agreement with @AMD to integrate their latest Instinct GPUs into our global infrastructure. With approximately 6GW of planned data center capacity dedicated to this deployment, we’re scaling our compute capacity to accelerate the d
items.why_it_matters: Meta signs a multi-year deal with AMD for ~6GW of data center capacity with Instinct GPUs — the largest non-NVIDIA AI infrastructure commitment yet, potentially reshaping the GPU market.

items.title: We expanded file input types so you can now pass docx, pptx, csv, xlsx, and more directly to the Res
items.raw_summary:
We expanded file input types so you can now pass docx, pptx, csv, xlsx, and more directly to the Responses API.

Your agents can now pull context from real-world files and generate more accurate outputs.

https://t.co/brKUsSfOJy https://t.co/no5zPgWgSv
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI Responses API now accepts docx, pptx, csv, xlsx natively — agents can finally process real-world business documents without preprocessing pipelines.
items.action: Remove document parsing middleware and use native file inputs

items.title: AI assistants like Claude can seem shockingly human—expressing joy or distress, and using anthropomo
items.raw_summary:
AI assistants like Claude can seem shockingly human—expressing joy or distress, and using anthropomorphic language to describe themselves. Why?

In a new post we describe a theory that explains why AIs act like humans: the persona selection model.

https://t.co/Gc3q0Dzq7Z
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic publishes a formal theory (persona selection model) explaining why AI systems exhibit human-like behaviors — important for anyone building user-facing AI products.
items.action: Read the persona selection model paper

items.title: The standard for frontier coding evals is changing with model maturity.
items.raw_summary:
The standard for frontier coding evals is changing with model maturity.

We now recommend reporting SWE-bench Pro and are sharing more detail on why we’re no longer reporting SWE-bench Verified as we work with the industry to establish stronger coding eval standards.

SWE-bench V
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI deprecates SWE-bench Verified in favor of SWE-bench Pro, signaling that current frontier models have saturated the old benchmark — eval standards are evolving fast.
items.action: Switch to SWE-bench Pro for coding agent evaluations

items.title: Introducing Claudia - A powerful GUI app and Toolkit for Claude Code.
items.raw_summary:
Introducing Claudia - A powerful GUI app and Toolkit for Claude Code.

Create custom agents, manage interactive Claude Code sessions, run secure background agents, and more.

✨ Features
- Interactive GUI Claude Code sessions.
- Checkpoints and reverting. (Yes, that one missing feature from Claude Code)
- Create and share custom agents.
- Run sandboxed background agents. (experimental)
- No-code MCP installation and configuration.
- Real-time Usage Dashboard.

Free and open-source. Get started at
items.why_it_matters: Claudia is a full GUI app for Claude Code with checkpoints, revert, custom agents, and sandboxed background agents — filling the most-requested missing features.
items.action: Try Claudia for checkpoint/revert in Claude Code sessions

items.title: New research: The AI Fluency Index.
items.raw_summary:
New research: The AI Fluency Index.

We tracked 11 behaviors across thousands of https://t.co/RxKnLNNcNR conversations—for example, how often people iterate and refine their work with Claude—to measure how well people collaborate with AI.

Read more: https://t.co/g65nGQFmjG
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic's AI Fluency Index tracks 11 collaboration behaviors across thousands of conversations, providing the first empirical framework for measuring human-AI collaboration quality.
items.action: Benchmark your team's AI collaboration patterns

items.title: 斯坦福大学竟然开了一门 Vibe Coding 课程
items.raw_summary:
斯坦福大学竟然开了一门 Vibe Coding 课程

课程编号 CS146S，全名叫《The Modern Software Developer》，持续 10 周，依次讲解了提示词工程、Agent 架构、MCP、上下文工程、安全攻防、Code Review、自动做 App 和上线运维。
https://t.co/n3tQVKD989 https://t.co/94m85Pgql9
items.why_it_matters: Stanford launches CS146S 'The Modern Software Developer' — a 10-week course covering prompt engineering, agent architecture, MCP, context engineering, and security, legitimizing AI-native development as a university discipline.
items.action: Review the CS146S syllabus for skill gaps

items.title: someone built a SELF-EVOLVING AI agent that rewrites its own code, prompts, and identity AUTONOMOUSL
items.raw_summary:
someone built a SELF-EVOLVING AI agent that rewrites its own code, prompts, and identity AUTONOMOUSLY, with having a background consciousness

its called OUROBOROS, open source, built by a russian PhD researcher who studies transformer internals, he built it as an experiment, it built everything else

it thinks on its own even when nobody is talking to it, each thought costs $0.07

when the researcher went to sleep at midnight, by 3:41am it mass produced 20 versions of itself, burned $2K in API
items.why_it_matters: OUROBOROS is an open-source self-evolving AI agent that rewrites its own code and prompts autonomously with background consciousness — a novel experiment in recursive self-improvement.
items.action: Study the OUROBOROS architecture for agent design ideas

items.why_it_matters: Open WebUI v0.8.5 adds voice dictation shortcuts and fixes model access controls — a solid incremental update for the leading open-source LLM frontend.
